turns.content:
In this assignment, you will create an annotated bibliography of 4 sources.
Select a topic that interests you. It should relate to human behaviour or society. This may be the same topic you researched for U1 A5, or it can be a new topic (e.g., drug addiction, immigration policies, juvenile delinquency, mental illness, high school graduation rates).

Use search engines and databases to find 4 resources related to this topic. At least one must be a journal article from a database.

Provide an annotation for each source.

Be sure that your annotated bibliography is in alphabetical order and that you are using correct APA format.

turns.speaker: model_b
turns.content:
Topic: Mental Health in College Students

Annotated Bibliography

Eisenberg, D., Golberstein, E., & Gollust, S. E. (2007). Help-seeking and access to mental health care in a university student population. Medical Care, 45(7), 594-601. https://doi.org/10.1097/MLR.0b013e31803bb4c1

This journal article, obtained from the Medical Care database, details a study on help-seeking behaviors and access to mental health care among university students. The authors found that despite the high prevalence of mental health issues among this population, help-seeking behavior was relatively low, and there were significant barriers to accessing care, such as stigma and lack of knowledge about available resources. This source provides valuable insight into the challenges faced by college students when it comes to seeking help for their mental health and emphasizes the importance of addressing these barriers to improve overall mental wellbeing on campuses.

Pedrelli, P., Nyer, M., Yeung, A., Zulauf, C., & Wilens, T. (2015). College Students: Mental Health Problems and Treatment Considerations. Academic Psychiatry, 39(5), 503–511. https://doi.org/10.1007/s40596-014-0205-9

This article provides an in-depth overview of the mental health problems most commonly experienced by college students, such as anxiety, depression, substance abuse, and eating disorders. The authors discuss the unique challenges and stressors that these students face and how these factors contribute to the development of mental health issues. This source is helpful in understanding the various mental health problems experienced by college students and suggests potential prevention efforts and treatment approaches that can be implemented within university settings.

Ross, A., Niebling, B., & Heckert, T. M. (1999). Sources of stress among college students. College Student Journal, 33(2), 312-317.

This study, found in the College Student Journal, explored the various sources of stress among college students and how they relate to negative mental health outcomes. The authors identified academic pressure, financial concerns, and time management issues as some of the most significant stressors experienced by this population. This source allows for a better understanding of the factors that contribute to the high rates of mental health issues among college students and provides valuable context for developing interventions and support systems.

Sontag-Padilla, L., Woodbridge, M. W., Mendelsohn, J., D'Amico, E. J., Osilla, K. C., Jaycox, L. H., Eberhart, N. K., Burnam, M. A., & Stein, B. D. (2016). Factors affecting mental health service utilization among California public college and university students. Psychiatric Services, 67(8), 890-897. https://doi.org/10.1176/appi.ps.201500307

In this article, the authors examine the factors that impact mental health service utilization among college and university students in California. The study found that general stigma around mental health, as well as students' perceptions of the quality and accessibility of services, played a significant role in determining whether they sought help for mental health issues. This source highlights the importance of addressing misconceptions and improving resources on campuses to encourage students to seek help when needed, thereby promoting a more supportive environment for maintaining mental wellbeing.

Topic: Drug Addiction

turns.speaker: model_b
turns.content:
Annotated Bibliography

Dole, V. P., & Nyswander, M. (1965). A medical treatment for diacetylmorphine (heroin) addiction: A clinical trial with methadone hydrochloride. JAMA, 193(8), 646-650. https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jama/article-abstract/1169569

This journal article from the Journal of the American Medical Association (JAMA) describes a groundbreaking clinical trial that introduced methadone as a medical treatment for heroin addiction. The authors' study demonstrates the effectiveness of methadone maintenance in reducing cravings, withdrawal symptoms, and overall rates of heroin use. This source provides a historical perspective on the development of drug addiction treatment methodologies and highlights the ongoing importance of research in finding effective medical interventions.

Frank, R. G., & Pollack, H. A. (2017). Addressing the fentanyl threat to public health. New England Journal of Medicine, 376(7), 605-607. https://doi.org/10.1056/NEJMp1615145

This article from the New England Journal of Medicine discusses the public health threat posed by fentanyl, a highly potent synthetic opioid. The authors argue that to address the increasing prevalence of fentanyl-related overdose deaths, comprehensive interventions should be instituted, including accurate public health data reporting, expanding access to medication-assisted treatment, and utilizing harm reduction strategies. This source provides valuable insight into the ongoing opioid crisis and potential policy measures for addressing the problem.

National Institute on Drug Abuse. (2020). Drugs, brains, and behavior: The science of addiction. https://www.drugabuse.gov/publications/drugs-brains-behavior-science-addiction

This resource from the National Institute on Drug Abuse provides an overview of the science behind drug addiction. It explains how repeated drug use affects the brain, leading to changes that can result in addiction and other harmful consequences. The source delves into risk factors, prevention strategies, and treatment options for addiction, making it a valuable tool for understanding the complexities involved in addressing drug addiction on an individual and societal level.

Volkow, N. D., Koob, G. F., & McLellan, A. T. (2016). Neurobiologic advances from the brain disease model of addiction. New England Journal of Medicine, 374(4), 363-371. https://doi.org/10.1056/NEJMra1511480

This article from the New England Journal of Medicine examines the latest advancements in understanding the neurobiology of addiction. The authors discuss how the brain disease model of addiction has led to a shift in thinking about the most effective interventions and treatment options available for people with substance use disorders. The source highlights the importance of integrating biological, psychological, and environmental factors when developing and implementing addiction treatment strategies.

turns.speaker: model_b
turns.content:
Topic: Immigration Policies and their Impact on Society

Annotated Bibliography

1. Borjas, G. J. (1994). The economics of immigration. Journal of Economic Literature, 32(4), 1667-1717. https://www.jstor.org/stable/2728791

This journal article from the Journal of Economic Literature presents a comprehensive review of the economic literature on immigration, discussing the impact of immigration on wages, employment, and income distribution in host countries. Borjas provides an analysis of various policies that have been implemented worldwide to address the economic consequences of immigration. This source is valuable in understanding the complex interplay between immigration policies and their effects on the economy and labor markets of host countries.

2. Card, D., Dustmann, C., & Preston, I. (2012). Immigration, wages, and compositional amenities. Journal of the European Economic Association, 10(1), 78-119. https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1542-4774.2011.01051.x

This journal article investigates the relationship between immigration and the wages of native workers in host countries. The authors' study concludes that although immigration can result in downward pressure on wages for some low-skilled workers, it can also lead to increased productivity and potential wage growth in different sectors. This source helps to provide a balanced view of the economic consequences of immigration policies.

3. Human Rights Watch. (2019). The human rights consequences of immigration policy. https://www.hrw.org/news/2019/06/19/human-rights-consequences-immigration-policy

This report from Human Rights Watch highlights the human rights consequences of immigration policies implemented in various countries around the world. The authors argue that many of these policies exacerbate the vulnerabilities of immigrants, leading to increased discrimination, marginalization, and human rights abuses. This source is crucial for understanding the impact of immigration policies on society from a human rights perspective.

4. Koser, K. (2014). International migration: A very short introduction (2nd Edition). Oxford University Press. https://biblioteca.co.oxfordjournals.org/content/intmigration/52/6/246.full.pdf

This book provides a comprehensive introduction to the study of international migration, including discussions of the causes, consequences, and political responses to global migration flows. It offers an examination of various immigration policies around the world, addressing both their intended and unintended effects. This source is helpful for obtaining a general understanding of immigration policies and their broader impact on society.

5. Migration Policy Institute. (2020). Migration policy and legislation. https://www.migrationpolicy.org/topics/migration-policy-legislation

This website from the Migration Policy Institute provides an extensive collection of reports, news articles, and policy briefs related to migration policy and legislation in different countries. The resources available on this site cover a wide range of topics, including asylum policies, family migration, temporary worker programs, and enforcement practices. This source serves as a valuable repository of information on various immigration policies and their impact on society.

6. United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR). (2018). Global trends: Forced displacement in 2017. https://www.unhcr.org/5b27be547.pdf

This report from the UNHCR provides statistics and analysis on forced displacement trends worldwide, including refugees, asylum seekers, and internally displaced persons. It serves as a reminder of the importance of sound immigration policies in addressing the needs of individuals forced to flee their homes due to war, persecution, or other threats to their safety. This source highlights the broader societal implications of immigration policies, particularly concerning humanitarian concerns and international obligations.

Topic: Drug Addiction

Annotated Bibliography

1. Dole, V. P., & Nyswander, M. (1965). A medical treatment for diacetylmorphine (heroin) addiction: A clinical trial with methadone hydrochloride. JAMA, 193(8), 646-650. https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jama/article-abstract/1169569

This journal article from the Journal of the American Medical Association (JAMA) describes a groundbreaking clinical trial that introduced methadone as a medical treatment for heroin addiction. The authors' study demonstrates the effectiveness of methadone maintenance in reducing cravings, withdrawal symptoms, and overall rates of heroin use. This source provides a historical perspective on the development of drug addiction treatment methodologies and highlights the ongoing importance of research in finding effective medical interventions.

2. Volkow, N. D., Koob, G. F., & McLellan, A. T. (2016). Neurobiologic advances from the brain disease model of addiction. New England Journal of Medicine, 374(4), 363-371. https://doi.org/10.1056/NEJMra1511480

This journal article from the New England Journal of Medicine examines the latest advancements in understanding the neurobiology of addiction. The authors discuss how the brain disease model of addiction has led to a shift in thinking about the most effective interventions and treatment options available for people with substance use disorders. The source highlights the importance of integrating biological, psychological, and environmental factors when developing and implementing addiction treatment strategies.

3. National Institute on Drug Abuse. (2020). Drugs, brains, and behavior: The science of addiction. https://www.drugabuse.gov/publications/drugs-brains-behavior-science-addiction

This resource from the National Institute on Drug Abuse provides an overview of the science behind drug addiction. It explains how repeated drug use affects the brain, leading to changes that can result in addiction and other harmful consequences. The source delves into risk factors, prevention strategies, and treatment options for addiction, making it a valuable tool for understanding the complexities involved in addressing drug addiction on an individual and societal level.

4. Partnership to End Addiction. (2021). Understanding addiction. https://drugfree.org/article/understanding-addiction/

This website offers information on the causes, risks, and effects of substance use disorders, as well as helpful resources for those affected by addiction. The site presents access to research findings, expert advice, and personal stories, emphasizing the importance of prevention, treatment, and recovery. This source is valuable for those seeking a deeper understanding of the multifaceted aspects of addiction, including its impact on individuals, families, and communities.

5. Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA). (2021). About medication-assisted treatment (MAT). https://www.samhsa.gov/medication-assisted-treatment/about

This website from the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA) provides information on medication-assisted treatment (MAT) for opioid use disorders. It explains the various medications available (e.g., methadone, buprenorphine, and naltrexone), how they work, and their role in a comprehensive treatment plan that includes counseling and support services. This source highlights the importance of evidence-based approaches in addressing drug addiction and discusses the benefits of MAT for individuals and communities.

6. World Health Organization (WHO). (2018). Drug use and health consequences. https://www.who.int/substance_abuse/facts/en/

This website from the World Health Organization (WHO) emphasizes the global impact of substance use and the health consequences related to drug addiction. The site offers information on the prevalence, risks, and health consequences associated with various drugs, as well as data on trends in drug-related deaths and infectious diseases associated with drug use. This source emphasizes the need for prevention, treatment, and harm reduction efforts on a global scale to address the significant public health implications of drug addiction.
